title: mentha × niliaca var. velutina
intro: Mentha × niliaca var. velutina, also known as velvet mint, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the mint family and is characterized by its soft, velvety leaves and purple flowers. The medicinal properties of velvet mint include its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of consuming or using velvet mint include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce stress and improve cognitive function. Velvet mint can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote overall well-being and relaxation.
